input.prompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

data.content:
Use the `IsTestProject` property — it's automatically set to `true` by the `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` package:

```xml
<!-- Directory.Build.props -->
<Project>
  <!-- shared properties for all projects … -->

  <PropertyGroup Condition="'$(IsTestProject)' == 'true'">
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

**Why it works:** Any project that references the `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` package gets `IsTestProject=true` set automatically. Your library projects never reference that package, so the condition is `false` and they remain libraries.

> **Tip:** If you need the condition evaluated later (after NuGet restore sets the property), move the block to a `Directory.Build.targets` file instead — targets are evaluated after imports/packages.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about how to conditionally set properties in a `Directory.Build.props` file so that `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP) runner properties only apply to test projects, not library/production projects.

This is a common pattern when migrating from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform. The key is using an MSBuild condition that detects whether a project is a test project.

The standard way to do this is to check for the `IsTestProject` property, which is set to `true` by the `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` package (or you can set it yourself). Alternatively, you can check if the project references `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`.


I should provide a practical example showing how to wrap these properties with a condition in the props file.
